CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, text_column VARCHAR(100));
SELECT left(text_column, 3) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT left(text_column, 5) FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE text_column LIKE '%DB%');
SELECT left(text_column, 3) AS short_text FROM (SELECT text_column FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2) AS subquery;
SELECT t1.id, left(t1.text_column, 4) AS left_text, t2.text_column FROM test_strings t1 JOIN test_strings t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.id <= 3;
SELECT id, left(text_column, 10) FROM (SELECT id, text_column FROM test_strings WHERE text_column IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 2) AS limited_strings;
SELECT DISTINCT left(text_column, 3) AS unique_prefix FROM test_strings;
